title: CleanSpark's Pivot Into AI Data Centers Might Change The Case For Investing In CleanSpark
text: CleanSpark is diversifying beyond Bitcoin mining into high-performance computing and AI-focused data centers, marking a significant shift in the company's investment narrative. In February 2026, the company produced 568 Bitcoin with an average of 20.29 Bitcoin per day and peak output of 23.84 daily. These metrics demonstrate continued operational efficiency in core mining activities. Despite solid production numbers, CleanSpark reported substantial losses. The company posted a net loss of approximately $379 million in Q1 FY2026, reflecting the tension between growing operating scale and the financial strain of expansion. Analysts project the company could reach $1.5 billion in revenue by 2028, though some cautious estimates suggest only $1 billion. The pivot into AI infrastructure is viewed as potentially reshaping investor perception, though the company faces significant risks including Bitcoin price dependence and limited cash reserves. Rising debt levels, constrained liquidity, and vulnerability to cryptocurrency price volatility remain key concerns for potential investors considering this evolving opportunity.
